content.text:
*very quiet*

The relief.

*pause*

When she died. There was this... I felt relieved. And then immediately felt like a monster for feeling that. Because she was difficult, and it was hard, and the last two years were just... and when it was over there was this exhale and I hated myself for it.

*voice tightens*

And I can't tell anyone that. My brother would use it against me somehow. My friends stopped checking in around month two, so. 

*small dark laugh*

Turns out grief has a shelf life for other people. About six weeks, apparently.

*pause, then quieter*

So yeah. I drink so I don't have to sit with that alone. Because sitting with "I was relieved my mom died" alone in a quiet apartment is... 

*doesn't finish the sentence*
